samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit full adder in combinational logic. 16-bit input operand A. 16-bit input operand B. Carry-in input. 16-bit output representing the sum of A and B. Carry-out output.

Design a 16-bit full adder using a combination of half adders and full adders.

One possible implementation is to use a 1-bit full adder to perform the addition of the least significant bits of A and B, and then to use the carry-out output of that adder to add the next bits of A and B, and so on.

Another possible implementation is to use a 2-bit carry-lookahead adder to perform the addition of the least significant two bits of A and B, and then to use the carry-out output of that adder to add the next two bits of A and B, and so on.

Implement the adder that best suits your needs.
